title: salix phylicifolia var. incana
intro: Salix phylicifolia var. incana, also known as the prickly willow, is a shrub that belongs to the willow family (Salicaceae) and is native to Europe. The bark and leaves of this plant have been traditionally used for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it effective in treating wounds and reducing pain. The health benefits of Salix phylicifolia var. incana include its ability to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms of arthritis, as well as its antiseptic properties, which can aid in wound healing. The herbal preparations of this plant include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used topically or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
